name: metadata-any
note: task_class 'payment' is one of the values listed in the rule, so metadata any matches.

messages.content: how do I return this order?

name: metadata-equals
note: consent is exactly 'denied', so metadata equals matches.

name: metadata-equals-no-match
note: consent 'denieddd' is not exactly 'denied', so metadata equals does not match (it is exact, not substring/prefix); the request falls through to default (cloud).

name: metadata-exists-false
note: No consent key is supplied, so metadata exists:false matches.

name: metadata-whitespace-counts-absent
note: A whitespace-only consent value counts as absent, so exists:false still matches.

name: metadata-any-comma-separated
note: The comma value 'billing, payment' is split into tokens; 'payment' is listed, so metadata any matches.

name: metadata-equals-case-sensitive
note: metadata equals is case-sensitive: 'DENIED' does not equal 'denied', so the rule misses and it falls through to default (cloud).

name: metadata-exists-true
note: A present, non-blank region value makes exists:true match.

name: metadata-trace
note: route_trace:true records the metadata leaf under condition 'metadata'; the rule's any short-circuits on the first (matching) child, so only one entry is traced.
